growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Wei Li et al. Microbiome processing of organic nitrogen input supports growth and cyanotoxin production of Microcystis aeruginosa cultures. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae082. [PMC11126159]
growth_records.snippet: The unaltered LE3 culture (xenic) only exhibited increases in chlorophyll autofluorescence with high nitrate, with higher rate of chlorophyll fluorescence (0.23 d-1) than PCC7806 (0.16 d-1 ...), but grew slower on low nitrate (0.02 d-1)
growth_records.notes: LE3 high-nitrate growth rate 0.23 d-1. Converted: 0.23/24 = 0.009583 h-1; doubling = 0.693/0.23 = 3.01 d = 72.3 h. LE3 is a Microcystis sp. (prokaryote).

growth_records.cite: Wei Li et al. Microbiome processing of organic nitrogen input supports growth and cyanotoxin production of Microcystis aeruginosa cultures. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae082. [PMC11126159]
growth_records.snippet: but grew slower on low nitrate (0.02 d-1) and decreased in abundance
growth_records.notes: LE3 low-nitrate (50 uM) growth rate 0.02 d-1. Converted: 0.02/24 = 0.000833 h-1; doubling = 0.693/0.02 = 34.65 d = 831.6 h.
